Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing showed no effect on root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones; enzyme preferentially uses 4′-hydroxylated flavanones, implying flux may favor aerial flavone branch rather than root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment did not significantly alter SbFNSII-1 transcript levels in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Limited direct value for increasing root-specific bioactive 4′-deoxyflavones; useful for understanding branch partitioning between standard flavones and root-specific flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided gene identification; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; RNAi in hairy roots; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro enzyme assay and kinetics.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes conversion of pinocembrin to chrysin and naringenin to apigenin in vitro, but is not required for accumulation of major root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP93B24; broadly active FNSII resembling typical Lamiales FNSIIs; likely contributes more to 4′-hydroxyflavone formation than to root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-1 encodes a CYP93B flavone synthase II with broad flavanone substrate specificity. 2. Recombinant enzyme converted pinocembrin, naringenin, and eriodictyol to chrysin, apigenin, and luteolin, respectively. 3. RNAi silencing in S. baicalensis hairy roots did not reduce baicalin, wogonoside, baicalein, or wogonin, indicating it is not the main enzyme for root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Relatively low and approximately equally distributed across roots, stems, leaves, and flowers; not induced by MeJA in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chrysin-derived 4′-deoxyflavones are not primarily controlled by this isoform; in vitro products include chrysin, apigenin, and luteolin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive flavones in Scutellaria roots have reported antioxidant, antiviral, and antitumor activities; this isoform is not the principal determinant of their root accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter in S. baicalensis; GAL-inducible yeast expression vector for assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the C2=C3 double bond in flavanones, converting pinocembrin to chrysin, naringenin to apigenin, and eriodictyol to luteolin in an NADPH-dependent microsomal reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1 (in WAT11 assay system)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots for 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones, though this gene itself is not root-enriched

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specialization for pinocembrin is associated with lower catalytic activity than SbFNSII-1; silencing causes accumulation of reduced/glycosylated flavanone derivatives such as pinocembrin-O-glucuronide and dihydrowogonin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strongly induced by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots; functional complementation in Arabidopsis required exogenous pinocembrin feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High value for metabolic engineering and medicinal quality improvement because it is a core determinant of chrysin and downstream bioactive 4′-deoxyflavones including baicalein/wogonin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided discovery; qRT-PCR tissue and MeJA profiling; RNAi in hairy roots; yeast microsome enzyme assay and kinetics; in vivo yeast feeding; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically converts pinocembrin to chrysin and is essential for root accumulation of baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP93B25; cannot use naringenin or eriodictyol; root-enriched expression mirrors root-specific flavone accumulation; a neofunctionalized FNSII specialized for 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-2 is highly expressed in roots and induced 4.8-fold by MeJA, correlating with root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. RNAi silencing markedly reduced baicalin, wogonoside, baicalein, and wogonin and caused accumulation of pinocembrin-related derivatives. 3. Recombinant SbFNSII-2 specifically converted pinocembrin to chrysin in vitro and in vivo, and transgenic Arabidopsis produced chrysin only when supplied with pinocembrin. 4. Together with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2, SbFNSII-2 reconstructed the core 4′-deoxyflavone pathway in tobacco leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots; 8-, 28-, and 36-fold higher in roots than in stems, leaves, and flowers, respectively; strongly induced by MeJA in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chrysin leading to baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root-specific bioactive flavones with reported antioxidant, antiviral, apoptosis-inducing, and antitumor activities.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter in S. baicalensis; CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana assays; GAL-inducible yeast expression vector for enzyme studies
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH-dependent desaturation of pinocembrin to form the flavone chrysin by introducing the C2=C3 double bond.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1 (in WAT11 assay system); functionally paired with SbCLL-7; SbCHS-2; SbCHI in pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Recruitment of cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA may compete with cinnamate 4-hydroxylase (C4H) and alter phenylpropanoid flux; expression in Arabidopsis alone did not yield pinocembrin because endogenous CHS could not efficiently use cinnamoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: No significant induction by MeJA in hairy roots; Arabidopsis feeding with cinnamic acid did not produce pinocembrin unless additional Scutellaria pathway genes were supplied in tobacco reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High value for bioengineering 4′-deoxyflavone production because it establishes the dedicated cinnamoyl-CoA supply needed to bypass the canonical 4-coumarate route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; recombinant protein purification from E. coli; substrate specificity and kinetic assays; RNAi in hairy roots; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient Nicotiana pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA and is required for full accumulation of root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains C-terminal SKL peroxisomal targeting motif; specific for cinnamic acid and inactive with 4-coumaric acid and caffeic acid; likely recruited from a CoA ligase-like ancestor rather than canonical 4CL duplication.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-7 is root-preferentially expressed in a pattern matching root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. Purified recombinant SbCLL-7 showed high specificity for cinnamic acid and no detectable activity with 4-coumaric acid or caffeic acid. 3. RNAi silencing reduced baicalin, wogonoside, and baicalein levels in hairy roots. 4. In tobacco leaves, SbCLL-7 together with SbCHS-2 enabled pinocembrin formation, establishing its role in supplying cinnamoyl-CoA to the specialized pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; branch point; committed specialized precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highest in roots; transcript levels in aerial organs at least threefold lower than in roots; not significantly changed by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive root flavones with antioxidant, antiviral, and antitumor-associated activities.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter in S. baicalensis; CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis/Nicotiana assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Ligates coenzyme A to cinnamic acid to form cinnamoyl-CoA, providing the dedicated starter substrate for pinocembrin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functionally paired with SbCHS-2; SbCHI; SbFNSII-2 in transient pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specialization toward cinnamoyl-CoA may redirect carbon from canonical flavonoid branches; no direct standalone enzymatic kinetics reported in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strongly induced by MeJA; in Nicotiana, pinocembrin formation by SbCHS-2 with SbCLL-7 occurred with or without cinnamic acid supplementation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High value for reconstructing and engineering 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis because it enables formation of pinocembrin from cinnamoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression comparison between roots and flowers; qRT-PCR tissue and MeJA profiling; transient expression with SbCLL-7 ± SbCHI ± SbFNSII-2 in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required specialized CHS isoform for pinocembrin formation and thereby for chrysin/4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly root enriched; closely related to CHS genes from Scutellaria species producing root-specific flavones; likely an allele of previously reported SbCHS2a/SbCHS-C/SbCHS2b-related sequences.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-2 is highly expressed in roots but low in flowers, matching the specialized root flavone pathway. 2. Co-expression of SbCHS-2 with SbCLL-7 in tobacco leaves was sufficient to produce pinocembrin, showing that endogenous tobacco CHS could not substitute. 3. Addition of SbFNSII-2 enabled chrysin formation, confirming SbCHS-2 as a core enzyme in the specialized 4′-deoxyflavone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots; low in flowers; strongly induced by MeJA (~16-fold).
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pinocembrin leading to chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive root flavones with medicinal antioxidant, antiviral, and antitumor properties.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native promoter in S. baicalensis; CaMV 35S promoter in Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to generate pinocembrin chalcone, which is then isomerized to pinocembrin.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functionally paired with SbCLL-7; SbCHI; SbFNSII-2 in pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Predominant expression in flowers suggests role in aerial/flower flavonoid biosynthesis rather than root-specific medicinal flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Low direct value for enhancing root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones but informative for distinguishing canonical versus specialized CHS isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression comparison; qRT-PCR tissue profiling; phylogenetic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Flower-enriched CHS isoform unlikely to be the principal enzyme for root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specifically expressed in flowers; only low transcript abundance in roots; included as contrasting CHS paralog to SbCHS-2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-1 is highly expressed in flowers and weakly expressed in roots. 2. Its expression pattern contrasts with the root-specific SbCHS-2, indicating functional partitioning after duplication. 3. The study does not provide direct evidence that SbCHS-1 contributes to root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Very high in flowers; low in roots; induced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes chalcone formation in the canonical flavonoid pathway, but specific substrate preference was not experimentally tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: The study suggests a unique SbCHI isoform is not required; spontaneous chalcone isomerization or endogenous host CHI can substitute in reconstruction assays.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in Nicotiana pathway reconstruction; adding SbCHI did not increase pinocembrin relative to SbCLL-7 + SbCHS-2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Low to moderate; less critical than SbCLL-7, SbCHS-2, and SbFNSII-2 because a specialized CHI was not required for the root-specific pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq transcript identification; cloning; transient Nicotiana pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not identified as a specialized determinant of root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis; endogenous CHI activity or spontaneous isomerization sufficed for pinocembrin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one CHI transcript was identified and appeared allelic to known CHIs; no evidence for a novel root-specific CHI isoform.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only a single CHI transcript was identified in S. baicalensis roots, with no evidence for a novel specialized isoform. 2. In tobacco reconstruction, SbCHI was not required for pinocembrin or chrysin formation beyond the effects of SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 ± SbFNSII-2. 3. The study concludes that a specific CHI activity is not essential for the specialized 4′-deoxyflavone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: A single transcript identified in roots; detailed organ expression not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pinocembrin leading to chrysin and downstream 4′-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pinocembrin is the central precursor for specialized bioactive root flavones.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereospecific isomerization of pinocembrin chalcone to pinocembrin, although spontaneous isomerization may also occur.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functionally tested with SbCLL-7; SbCHS-2; SbFNSII-2 in pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Preferential activation of 4-coumaric and caffeic acids suggests flux toward lignin/general phenylpropanoid metabolism rather than specialized 4′-deoxyflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transcript levels significantly enhanced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Low for direct bioengineering of root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones; useful as a comparator for avoiding diversion of cinnamic acid away from the desired branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; qRT-PCR tissue and MeJA profiling; recombinant protein purification from E. coli; substrate specificity and kinetic assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad CoA ligase preferring 4-coumaric acid and caffeic acid, not a dedicated supplier for the root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 4-Coumaric acid; caffeic acid; low-efficiency cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped with canonical Arabidopsis 4CLs; likely involved in lignin/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis rather than specialized flavone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-1 is expressed mainly in stems and roots. 2. Recombinant SbCLL-1 activated cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, and caffeic acid, but showed much higher catalytic efficiency for 4-coumaric and caffeic acids than for cinnamic acid. 3. The enzyme is unlikely to be the dedicated CoA ligase for root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highest in stems, followed by roots; low in leaves and flowers; induced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids by ligating CoA, with strongest activity toward 4-coumaric acid and caffeic acid and weak activity toward cinnamic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399

Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Favors 4-coumarate branch and may divert precursor away from cinnamoyl-CoA-dependent 4′-deoxyflavone production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transcript levels significantly enhanced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Low direct value for enhancing root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones; relevant for understanding competing phenylpropanoid activation routes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; qRT-PCR tissue and MeJA profiling; recombinant protein purification from E. coli; substrate specificity and kinetic assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad CoA ligase with high affinity for 4-coumaric acid and some activity toward cinnamic acid, but not specialized for 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically close to Arabidopsis 4CL3 and biochemically similar in substrate preference.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-5 is highly expressed in roots and stems. 2. Recombinant SbCLL-5 activated 4-coumaric acid most efficiently and also converted cinnamic acid and caffeic acid. 3. Compared with SbCLL-7, SbCLL-5 has much lower efficiency for cinnamic acid and is not the dedicated enzyme for the specialized root flavone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in roots and stems; lower in leaves and flowers; induced by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Ligates CoA to hydroxycinnamic acids, especially 4-coumaric acid, with lower efficiency toward cinnamic acid and caffeic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399

Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis
